stories.headline: Israel captures Beaufort Castle in deepest Lebanon ground push in 26 years; Hezbollah fires 50+ rockets
stories.summary: Israel's Golani Brigade seized the 12th-century Beaufort Castle and its strategic ridge in southern Lebanon on May 31, with Prime Minister Netanyahu calling it a 'dramatic shift' and ordering operations beyond the Litani River. Hezbollah responded with more than 50 rockets and drones into Galilee towns; France called an emergency UN Security Council meeting.

perspectives.keyFraming: Strategic/military framing: the deepest Israeli ground advance into Lebanon since 2000 and a de-facto rupture of the April ceasefire.
perspectives.summary: AP/Reuters and CNN treat the Beaufort capture as the deepest ground push in 26 years and an open break with April's truce. CNN highlights Netanyahu's order to advance beyond the Litani River and notes 1.2 million Lebanese have been displaced since March.

sources.title: Israel seizes Crusader-era castle as Netanyahu orders forces deeper into Lebanon
Israel seizes medieval castle as it expands major offensive in southern Lebanon
Israel Troops Capture Beaufort Castle in Southern Lebanon Push Against Hezbollah

perspectives.keyFraming: European framing: Macron's blunt condemnation of the escalation and Paris's push for both an emergency UNSC session and a US-Iran deal.
perspectives.summary: France 24 and Euronews report Macron's statement that 'nothing justifies the major escalation under way in south Lebanon' and his call for an emergency UN Security Council meeting. Paris paired condemnation with renewed pressure for a US-Iran agreement to defuse the wider regional conflict.

sources.title: Israel seizes strategic Beaufort Castle in Lebanon, issues new evacuation orders
Israel retakes Beaufort castle in Lebanon, Paris issues strong condemnation

perspectives.keyFraming: Arab/regional framing: emphasizes fresh Israeli displacement orders, civilian impact, and continued ceasefire violations.
perspectives.summary: Al Jazeera centers the displacement orders Israel issued for additional villages, framing the castle's capture as part of an ongoing pattern of ceasefire violations rather than a one-off operational achievement. The piece highlights residents fleeing under evacuation orders across multiple villages.

sources.title: Israel issues more displacement orders in Lebanon, seizes strategic castle

stories.headline: Trump withholds final approval on 60-day Iran ceasefire framework as Hegseth signals strike readiness
stories.summary: President Trump exited weekend Situation Room meetings without a 'final determination' on a tentative 60-day US-Iran ceasefire extension, saying he is 'in no hurry' and sending the draft back with tougher uranium-enrichment and Strait of Hormuz terms. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told the Shangri-La Dialogue the US military remains 'more than capable' of resuming strikes if talks collapse.

perspectives.keyFraming: Frames Trump's eleventh-hour demands as risking a tentative deal while gas prices stay elevated and Iran's enrichment stockpile grows.
perspectives.summary: Left-leaning coverage stresses the economic and humanitarian cost of stalling: elevated gas, an IAEA report on Iranian uranium stockpiles, and Sen. Chris Murphy calling the conflict 'an absolute disaster for the United States' on Face the Nation. The frame is that ending the war should be the immediate priority.

sources.title: Transcript: Sen. Chris Murphy on Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan, May 31, 2026

perspectives.keyFraming: Tracks the unsigned memorandum of understanding mechanics: Hormuz passage, mine clearance, sanctions waivers, frozen-asset language.
perspectives.summary: Center outlets report the draft as 'relief for performance' and note Qatar's shuttle diplomacy continues while a US Navy port blockade has stopped 118 vessels. Trump said Saturday he is 'in no hurry' and exited weekend meetings without an announcement.

sources.title: U.S. and Iran still without deal to end war after Trump says he's not in a 'hurry'
US military ready to resume combat in Gulf if needed, defense secretary warns

perspectives.keyFraming: Casts Trump as the disciplined negotiator refusing to ease leverage prematurely; warns against an 'economic bailout' of the Iranian regime.
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning outlets emphasize Trump's vow to 'finish the job' if talks fail and a Wall Street Journal editorial warning that lifting the naval blockade before nuclear dismantlement 'would be the real betrayal.' Hegseth's Singapore remarks are framed as restoring deterrence.

sources.title: Trump warns Iran US will 'finish the job' if deal collapses as Israel expands Lebanon offensive
Wall Street Journal board warns Trump against 'economic bailout' in Iran deal

stories.headline: FEMA enters hurricane season weakened after year of Noem-Lewandowski rule
stories.summary: On the eve of the June 1 start of Atlantic hurricane season, a CNN investigation detailed how Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem and adviser Corey Lewandowski paralyzed FEMA over a year, stalling more than $15 billion in disaster funds and driving out roughly 20% of the workforce. Trump has tapped former acting administrator Cameron Hamilton to lead the agency a second time.

perspectives.keyFraming: Ties FEMA's hollowing-out directly to Noem and Lewandowski as record-warm seas threaten an 'extremely active' season.
perspectives.summary: Left-leaning coverage links the workforce exodus and stalled grants to Noem and Lewandowski's management, citing a DHS Inspector General probe into contract handling. MSNBC and E&E News frame Cameron Hamilton's reinstatement as arriving too late to rescue the season.

sources.title: As hurricane season begins, Trump's FEMA chief once again fails to inspire confidence
'Holding our breath': Hurricane season is here, and FEMA is shorthanded

perspectives.keyFraming: CNN investigation traces FEMA's gutting to a 'shadow administrator' arrangement now under DHS watchdog probe.
perspectives.summary: Center reporting documents $15B in unspent disaster aid and a ~20% workforce decline. CNN highlights Trump's renomination of Cameron Hamilton — the Navy SEAL fired in May 2025 for telling Congress he opposed abolishing FEMA — as a tacit admission the agency had been mismanaged.

sources.title: The race to fix FEMA after a year of chaos tore it apart

stories.headline: Anthropic ships Claude Opus 4.8 with Dynamic Workflows; closes $65B round at $965B; promises Mythos-class rollout
stories.summary: On May 28-29 Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.8 (with new effort-level controls and a research-preview Dynamic Workflows feature that orchestrates parallel subagents). The company simultaneously closed a $65B round at a $965B valuation and said its Mythos-class models — currently restricted to Project Glasswing partners Amazon, Microsoft, and Apple for cybersecurity use — will be available to all customers in the coming weeks.

perspectives.keyFraming: Anthropic now leads valuation tables and is openly gating its most powerful model behind cybersecurity safety partners — a new tier of frontier rollout.
perspectives.summary: Coverage notes 4.8 ships at unchanged Opus pricing; Dynamic Workflows is a clear step toward agentic 'plan-execute-verify' patterns inside Claude Code. The Mythos disclosure is notable for what it implies: a model class where Anthropic believes wider release requires deliberate staging because of dual-use cybersecurity capability. The $965B valuation now exceeds OpenAI's last reported mark.

sources.title: Anthropic leapfrogs OpenAI with a record $965 billion valuation and says its 'Mythos' AI model is coming soon
Anthropic launches Claude Opus 4.8, prepares Mythos-class models for all customers

stories.headline: Dow closes above 51,000, S&P 500 logs ninth straight weekly gain as AI rally + Iran ceasefire optimism cap record May
stories.summary: Friday May 29 capped a strong month: Dow Industrials +0.72% to 51,032.46, S&P 500 +0.22% to 7,580.06 (ninth consecutive weekly gain, longest streak since 2023), and Nasdaq Composite +0.26% to 26,972.62. For the month, Nasdaq jumped roughly 8%, S&P 500 about 5%, and Dow about 3%, driven by Dell's AI-server earnings and growing optimism on the 60-day US-Iran ceasefire framework.

perspectives.keyFraming: Records cap the strongest tech month since the post-pandemic rally, with AI demand and the Iran framework driving simultaneous risk-on bid.
perspectives.summary: Reuters/Yahoo Finance and TheStreet lead the Friday close: Dow above 51,000, S&P 500 7,580.06, Nasdaq 26,972.62; ninth weekly gain for the S&P is the longest streak since 2023. Drivers were Dell's earnings beat (stock surged up to 40%) plus White House signals it would soon endorse the Iran framework. Treasury yields drifted lower (10-year ~4.45%), supportive of multiples.

sources.title: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq cap winning month with fresh records in AI-fueled rally as Trump nears Iran decision
Stock Market Today (May 29, 2026): S&P 500 good for ninth consecutive week of gains
